decks.notes: The deck focuses on using control elements such as Rishadan Port, Mox Diamond, and Sphere of Resistance to disrupt the opponent's strategy while leveraging Oath of Druids to summon powerful creatures like Terravore and Treetop Village onto the battlefield. The main goal is to maintain board control with cards like Wasteland and Mishra's Factory, while also gaining card advantage with Sylvan Library. The deck also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Naturalize to handle threats, making it a versatile and resilient control archetype.

The deck focuses on quickly and efficiently playing Phyrexian Dreadnought in combination with Stifle to create a powerful combo that can potentially win the game in a single turn. The deck also includes various counterspells and card draw spells to help protect the combo and find the necessary pieces. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up the game-winning combo as quickly as possible.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with creatures using cards like Duress, Cabal Therapy, and Reanimate, then use Hermit Druid to dump the majority of the deck into the graveyard. This sets up the combo with Cephalid Illusionist and Nomads en-Kor to mill the remaining cards and create a massive creature in the form of Sutured Ghoul. The deck also includes various mana-fixing lands to ensure consistency with the combo.

This deck focuses on utilizing enchantments to control the board and generate card advantage. By using cards like Enchantress's Presence and Mirri's Guile to draw extra cards, the deck can set up powerful combos with cards like Parallax Wave and Opalescence. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with enchantments while maintaining control of the game with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. Serra's Sanctum provides additional mana acceleration to help fuel the deck's powerful enchantment-based strategy. Overall, this deck aims to create a dominant board presence through the synergy of its enchantments.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with Enchantresses and enchantments like Wild Growth, then using powerful combo pieces like Opalescence and Parallax Wave to control the board and overwhelm the opponent. The deck also utilizes cards like Serra's Sanctum and Replenish to recur important enchantments and maintain a strong board presence. By drawing cards with Enchantress's Presence and manipulating the top of the library with Mirri's Guile, the deck aims to set up powerful plays and ultimately outvalue the opponent through enchantment synergy.

The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to create infinite mana and then win the game. It utilizes cards like Hermit Druid and Survival of the Fittest to search for key creatures like Phyrexian Devourer and Triskelion. Once the combo is assembled, the deck can generate infinite mana and use it to pump Triskelion's damage or exile its own deck with Phyrexian Devourer for a lethal attack. The deck also includes disruption elements like Cabal Therapy to protect the combo and ensure a smooth execution.

The Mono Black Aggro deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's hand and graveyard while applying pressure with efficient and evasive creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade. With a mix of hand disruption spells like Cabal Therapy and Duress, removal spells like Smother and Diabolic Edict, and powerful threats that can quickly close out the game, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. The inclusion of Mishra's Factory and Wasteland provides additional utility and disruption, allowing for a versatile and aggressive gameplan.

The deck focuses on using a combination of cards like Vision Charm, Phyrexian Dreadnought, and Stifle to quickly assemble a powerful combo that can overwhelm opponents. By controlling the game with counterspells and card draw, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its win condition. The inclusion of cards like Peek and Portent helps the player dig for key combo pieces, while cards like Flash of Insight and Gush provide additional card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to be proactive in executing its combo strategy while keeping opponents on the back foot.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly play out creatures, generate mana with cards like Priest of Titania and Gaea's Cradle, and utilize cards like Natural Order and Survival of the Fittest to tutor for powerful creatures like Deranged Hermit. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of elves and other creatures, while also having the ability to disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Nantuko Vigilante and Wirewood Symbiote. Overall, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and maintain board presence throughout the game.

The main focus of this deck is to use the powerful combo of Opalescence and Replenish to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By filling the graveyard with enchantments using cards like Attunement and Frantic Search, the deck can easily bring back a large number of enchantments with Replenish, turning them all into creatures with Opalescence. This can create a sudden and powerful board presence that is difficult for opponents to overcome, making it a key strategy for winning games in a Premodern 1 vs 1 format.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the powerful combo of Replenish and Opalescence to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By using cards like Intuition and Attunement to fill the graveyard with enchantments, the player can then play Replenish to bring all enchantments back onto the battlefield, turning them into creatures with Opalescence. Cards like Parallax Wave and Decree of Silence provide control elements to keep the opponent in check while the combo pieces are assembled. This deck aims to create a game-winning board state through its combo synergy.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo pieces needed to win the game by utilizing the powerful card Replenish. By filling the graveyard with enchantments using Attunement and Frantic Search, the player can then cast Replenish to bring back all enchantments from the graveyard to the battlefield, including powerful cards like Parallax Wave and Opalescence. This allows for the player to generate significant advantage and potentially lock out their opponent from being able to effectively play the game.

This deck focuses on using Ill-Gotten Gains to return key combo pieces from the graveyard, such as Lotus Petal, Lion's Eye Diamond, and Brain Freeze. It relies on cards like Cabal Ritual and Dark Ritual to generate mana quickly and efficiently, while also using cards like Intuition to tutor for specific combo pieces. The deck also includes disruption tools like Tormod's Crypt and Chain of Vapor to handle opposing threats. The goal is to combo off quickly and mill the opponent's entire library with Brain Freeze to win the game.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the Tinker combo with Fling to quickly and efficiently deal damage to the opponent. By sacrificing creatures like Phyrexian Devourer to Fling, while also using cards like Altar of Dementia and Mind Stone to generate mana and draw cards, the deck aims to create a deadly combo that can win the game in just a few turns. With additional cards like City of Brass and Tsabo's Web to support the combo, this deck focuses on a fast and aggressive playstyle to overwhelm the opponent.

The main idea of this deck is to assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to quickly get a 12/12 creature on the battlefield for only one mana. The deck includes various card draw spells and counterspells to protect the combo and control the game. The inclusion of cards like Flash of Insight and Vision Charm helps with finding the combo pieces quickly. Overall, the deck aims to win the game by efficiently playing and protecting the combo while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The central idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle, using cheap and efficient burn spells and creatures to continuously deal damage. The deck focuses on maintaining pressure and preventing the opponent from stabilizing, with cards like Sulfuric Vortex providing a constant source of damage. The inclusion of Grim Lavamancer and Mogg Fanatic further enhances the deck's ability to deal damage quickly and efficiently. Overall, the deck aims to close out the game quickly before the opponent can establish a strong defense.
